title: Scalp Cooling in Preventing Persistent Chemotherapy-Induced Alopecia: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
doi: Scalp Cooling in Preventing Persistent Chemotherapy-Induced Alopecia: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
hypothesis_for_validation: Scalp cooling during chemotherapy reduces the incidence and severity of persistent chemotherapy-induced alopecia at 6 months after chemotherapy completion compared with usual care.
future_work_for_validation: Further research is necessary to observe longer-term benefits of scalp cooling beyond 6 months.

title: Autologous vs. implant-based breast reconstruction and long-term mental disorder risk: a retrospective cohort study emulating a target trial.
doi: Autologous vs. implant-based breast reconstruction and long-term mental disorder risk: a retrospective cohort study emulating a target trial.
hypothesis_for_validation: AR would be associated with a higher long-term risk of clinically diagnosed mental disorders compared with IBR at 1, 5, and up to 9 years after reconstruction.
future_work_for_validation: Integration of mental health screening and age-sensitive, individualized counseling into preoperative decision-making; replication in other healthcare systems.

title: Less harmful alternative for smoking: addressing concerns on the observational study of electronic cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes.
doi: Less harmful alternative for smoking: addressing concerns on the observational study of electronic cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes.
future_work_for_validation: Improved observational designs (clearer exposure definitions, handling of dual use, causal-inference frameworks) and long-term cohorts to clarify cardiovascular risk of e-cigarettes.
